title: 18 U.S.C. 1111 Murder
text:
Title: 18 U.S.C. section 1111 Murder
Citation: 18 U.S.C. section 1111
Jurisdiction: us
Source URL: https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/USCODE-2023-title18/pdf/USCODE-2023-title18-partI-chap51-sec1111.pdf

Section 1111(a) defines murder as the unlawful killing of a human being with malice aforethought within the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States. The section lists first degree murder categories including murder perpetrated by poison, lying in wait, or other willful, deliberate, malicious, and premeditated killing, and murder committed in specified felony circumstances.

Section 1111(b) provides that whoever is guilty of murder in the first degree shall be punished by death or by imprisonment for life; whoever is guilty of murder in the second degree shall be imprisoned for any term of years or for life.
importance_reason: primary US federal statute for murder definition and sentencing
